content.text: I'll help you deploy a PHP application on Mittwald. Let me start by listing your available projects, then create a PHP app with a MySQL database.

input.description: List Mittwald projects and create PHP app
input.prompt:
Use the Mittwald MCP tools to:

1. First, list all available projects using mcp__mittwald__mittwald_project_list_projects
2. Identify which projects are writable (look for "MCP Server Dev" organization or project "p-b3a3k3")
3. If a writable project exists, use it. Otherwise, create a new project on server "s-igc7dy" using mcp__mittwald__mittwald_project_create_project
4. Create a PHP 8.2 app installation using mcp__mittwald__mittwald_app_install_app with:
   - PHP 8.2 runtime
   - Document root: /public (or /htdocs if preferred)
5. Create a MySQL database using mcp__mittwald__mittwald_database_create_mysql_database
6. Create a MySQL database user using mcp__mittwald__mittwald_database_create_mysql_user with appropriate permissions

Return a summary with:
- Project ID and name used
- App installation details (ID, domain, PHP version)
- Database details (name, host)
- Database user credentials
- Next steps for deploying the PHP application

content.text:
Use the Mittwald MCP tools to:

1. First, list all available projects using mcp__mittwald__mittwald_project_list_projects
2. Identify which projects are writable (look for "MCP Server Dev" organization or project "p-b3a3k3")
3. If a writable project exists, use it. Otherwise, create a new project on server "s-igc7dy" using mcp__mittwald__mittwald_project_create_project
4. Create a PHP 8.2 app installation using mcp__mittwald__mittwald_app_install_app with:
   - PHP 8.2 runtime
   - Document root: /public (or /htdocs if preferred)
5. Create a MySQL database using mcp__mittwald__mittwald_database_create_mysql_database
6. Create a MySQL database user using mcp__mittwald__mittwald_database_create_mysql_user with appropriate permissions

Return a summary with:
- Project ID and name used
- App installation details (ID, domain, PHP version)
- Database details (name, host)
- Database user credentials
- Next steps for deploying the PHP application

Usage: claude mcp [options] [command]

Configure and manage MCP servers

Options:
  -h, --help                                     Display help for command

Commands:
  serve [options]                                Start the Claude Code MCP server
  add [options] <name> <commandOrUrl> [args...]  Add an MCP server to Claude Code.
  
  Examples:
    # Add HTTP server:
    claude mcp add --transport http sentry https://mcp.sentry.dev/mcp
  
    # Add SSE server:
    claude mcp add --transport sse asana https://mcp.asana.com/sse
  
    # Add stdio server:
    claude mcp add --transport stdio airtable --env AIRTABLE_API_KEY=YOUR_KEY -- npx -y airtable-mcp-server
  remove [options] <name>                        Remove an MCP server
  list                                           List configured MCP servers
  get <name>                                     Get details about an MCP server
  add-json [options] <name> <json>               Add an MCP server (stdio or SSE) with a JSON string
  add-from-claude-desktop [options]              Import MCP servers from Claude Desktop (Mac and WSL only)
  reset-project-choices                          Reset all approved and rejected project-scoped (.mcp.json) servers within this project
  help [command]                                 display help for command

Good! I can see the MCP server is configured for this project at `/Users/robert/Code/mittwald-mcp`. Now I understand the issue - when I re-added the server, it requires a new OAuth authentication. Let me check if there are MCP tools already available to me first, since this is an active conversation:

Let me check what the actual error is by looking at more recent logs and understanding the authentication flow. The issue is that the JWT token expired. Based on the CLAUDE.md instructions, I can see that the MCP server uses OAuth authentication through the bridge server.

Let me provide you with a comprehensive summary of the situation and what needs to be done:

---

## Summary: MCP Server Authentication Issue

### Current Situation

The Mittwald MCP server at `https://mittwald-mcp-fly2.fly.dev/mcp` is **healthy and running**, but authentication is failing due to an **expired JWT token**.

**Error from logs:**
```
[WARN] JWT verification failed { error: '"exp" claim timestamp check failed' }
```

### Root Cause

The OAuth token that Claude Code uses to authenticate with the MCP server has expired. The MCP server expects a valid JWT token from the OAuth bridge server (`mittwald-oauth-server.fly.dev`), but the token currently stored in Claude Code's configuration has expired.

### Solution Required

You need to **re-authenticate with the Mittwald MCP server** through the OAuth flow:

1. **I've already removed and re-added the MCP server** to your local configuration:
   ```bash
   claude mcp remove mittwald
   claude mcp add --transport http mittwald https://mittwald-mcp-fly2.fly.dev/mcp
   ```

2. **Next step: Trigger OAuth authentication**
   - You need to restart Claude Code or trigger a new session that will initiate the OAuth flow
   - The flow will:
     - Redirect you to the OAuth bridge server
     - Bridge redirects to Mittwald for authentication
     - After successful auth, you'll get a new JWT token
     - Token is stored in Claude Code's secure storage

### Alternative Approach

If you prefer to test the Mittwald tools manually without OAuth, you can:

1. **Use the Mittwald CLI directly** with your existing credentials
2. **Check if a direct API token approach** is available in the MCP server configuration

### What I Need From You

content.content:
Create a new MySQL database

USAGE
  $ mw database mysql create -d <value> --version <value> [--token <value>] [-p
    <value>] [-q] [--collation <value>] [--character-set <value>]
    [--user-password <value>] [--user-external] [--user-access-level
    full|readonly]

FLAGS
  -d, --description=<value>         (required) a description for the database
  -p, --project-id=<value>          ID or short ID of a project; this flag is
                                    optional if a default project is set in the
                                    context
  -q, --quiet                       suppress process output and only display a
                                    machine-readable summary
      --character-set=<value>       [default: utf8mb4] the character set to use
      --collation=<value>           [default: utf8mb4_unicode_ci] the collation
                                    to use
      --user-access-level=<option>  [default: full] the access level preset for
                                    the default user
                                    <options: full|readonly>
      --user-external               enable external access for default user
      --user-password=<value>       [env: MYSQL_PWD] the password to use for the
                                    default user
      --version=<value>             (required) the MySQL version to use

AUTHENTICATION FLAGS
  --token=<value>  API token to use for authentication (overrides environment
                   and config file). NOTE: watch out that tokens passed via this
                   flag might be logged in your shell history.

FLAG DESCRIPTIONS
  -p, --project-id=<value>

    ID or short ID of a project; this flag is optional if a default project is
    set in the context

    May contain a short ID or a full ID of a project; you can also use the "mw
    context set --project-id=<VALUE>" command to persistently set a default
    project for all commands that accept this flag.

  -q, --quiet

    suppress process output and only display a machine-readable summary

    This flag controls if you want to see the process output or only a summary.
    When using mw non-interactively (e.g. in scripts), you can use this flag to
    easily get the IDs of created resources for further processing.

  --version=<value>  the MySQL version to use
